# 2018 News

[![](images/photo_news_dec18_axmouth.png)](images/large_news_dec18_axmouth.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_dec18_axmouth.png)

Fisherman Charlie Ziemann from Axmouth fishing port in Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve demonstrating the use of his insulated fish boxes and ice onboard his boat as an immediate step to keep his fish iced.

These include two insulated boxes; one for slushed-ice to reduce core temperature quickly and the other filled with ice to keep the fish cool. Slushed-ice is crucial to reduce the core temperature of fish as soon as they are caught. This will increased their shelf-life and therefore their marketability.

As fishermen have agreed to a reduction in gear levels in the interests of the environment, it is therefore desirable to help them achieve an optimum price for their catch by way of providing basic ice facilities within their port.

The insulated boxes and chiller room facility providing ice and fish storage is part funded by the [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARCzTVSdHtlDubvkGqAhBWu_6MiCuNU_LD_E1SZUmIr3O0H8m4j1Ef9S7mLThtQsQUazvlVpQBF0VhcQ&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARCsAA2kEH8-5j-XBdfdjwa42W4Sh4p7-IqGoZcADO37yOH-sKC5ffouUHfgEEiMrAITvJH3jygL7i5JJ8IDuddsqgQZeYRnDYTpLtP1B-6MvIaWXHUB27GQXzu9-OkK-s16sldFxpdLS-ulIHxmv2XmtxdKD-LcmR2ISdF6ZCMlnN2PaXLPlr1Qi0Q84xtFQWDxr-7OWJUqe_xfABlftOdweLG7mji87rSq--Q219OAUUrM-TmGt-PySaYY2sLkaIan5BQMi4JhttHDLCVtJY_cS6bU9MM5vy_oSijTV-rEn_-Jtbwq1LIJqm9zhj0u-pGBZ3SwK74xwf1LWrYWwVWCoA) (facilitators of the Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve) and the European Fisheries Fund (EFF).

The fishing ports of Beer, Lyme Regis and West Bay all have their own chiller rooms and ice making facilities and is being used to great success.

For more information head over to: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_dec18_ggapp.png)](images/large_news_dec18_ggapp.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_dec18_ggapp.png)

Fisherman Jamie Smith from the port of West Bay within Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve pictured using his new tracking app, Gap Guardian, developed by [The AST Group](https://www.facebook.com/TheASTGroup/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARAvg95_JSKxkKtbdhVVEPeh77uHwKXtpciQ7Z_EhVMUzPQ2ZPEoikTCZ2s1EsamBEgJYFFO8sQC5F_9&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARDBYqYgzQWkgq2S0uwWRlHOIit7eT3RVPfwKOVqmBl0ZFRlEydR3h-kz6xywcW-CpTiYHTxmdW0mf7EkteVvD1FFgFtRj4zJvyoU_0_ycK8bj97L-DKVK6xWsm1YQnA-QKiTbn5j7q7BaQaDgClanZWS3VrIuOcTtV4Y-UvTFeGKUjyfxBrImij04RMvSqsd9gPlM2yJvW7qN5RxdxehCe42Biyvqn_utODwbTcBlFEhvdAHCHtLeN6VNA6XPR8P94QziuLiX8vFj8B0qPfMDAYR4BjKLYlJx0xrn3TyxbCVa8hJq50B6-zMDXv8i80ZcAnrk9BIWTovFnWIiIV3DiiNQ). The app tracks and monitors the position of our fishing vessels and ensures full traceability for Reserve Seafood.

For more details about Reserve Seafood, our sustainable, premium quality, provenance-assured seafood of Lyme Bay visit: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_dec18_fishfriday.png)](images/large_news_dec18_fishfriday.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_dec18_fishfriday.png)

Keep a look out for Dover Sole this month. This one was caught sustainably in Lyme Bay Reserve in a fixed net and is kept fresh in our chiller units. These beautiful flatfish are great pan-fried or roasted with butter. All part of Reserve Seafood, the sustainable, premium quality, provenance-assured seafood of Lyme Bay.

For more information head over to: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

![](images/photo_news_dec18_blog.png)

![](images/photo_news_dec18_blog.png)

Mark is a member of the Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve and skipper of his boats Hollie Rose and Dacky’s Cat. Mark has been fishing for the past 20 years. As a member of Reserve Seafood his top catches are by rod & line.

For further information about Reserve Seafood, the premium quality, provenance-assured seafood of Lyme Bay visit: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/)

Top feedback scores from a recent education outreach visit to Parrett and Axe Primary School in Beaminster, Dorset.

If you work in education in the Devon & Dorset area and would like a FREE school visit on the popular topics of Sustainable Fishing or Marine Litter please get in touch with our Education Officer: paula@bluemarinefoundation.com

For more information head over to: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/education/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/education/)

[![](images/photo_news_dec18_worldfisheriesday.png)](images/large_news_dec18_worldfisheriesday.png)

![](images/photo_news_dec18_worldfisheriesday.png)

Pictured center are our very own fishermen Gavin and Charlie Ziemann from Axmouth in Devon. [![](images/photo_news_nov18_thornback.png)](images/large_news_nov18_thornback.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_thornback.png)

An impressive Thornback Ray caught by Mark Dack during a fishing trip last Thursday out in Lyme Bay Reserve with [Superdry](https://www.facebook.com/Superdry/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARCgN_RVCNH5lRKhfvW_AHPlXRA9D8cUkQd13GGVYwokfdiT4eb-Zth1T6kqMDmPgvRMZDER6jX3nfSy&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAYnTKSgsxMEmlY5jX42TlmdxRsdO1mQ30cnHDfslIROhNDyecjrtbyKlUfPAN0uhvfSsWktSyseJnZ23KFkS5XNU5pWkwHZtNOZ9MNSZ67HoEA2m_AlIx1wfz5YTecDToBjKesqfnHxz4HhWkCsC-N2FRng947u1BmwpthyKef-IFOq_-o_HJ_X54OB3muQLrAN6wStzPQ1TwPFBAZXPpbDK2VltSJt6C7SomQ9J4wWstaVhSWh9BZICbrqyshTCuAMXThO5waQ8sJxazBF2ewz8BOOthUixrJ6kKFtZlAOfLeZfmCGXdkGlK-bV-GLfg8tAVNw3xYph3aGcNJeubVXw) co-founder Julian Dunkerton (pictured right) who was seeing firsthand the benefits experienced by both the local fishing community and the marine environment as a result of his generous donation to [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARBhNWEg3vE99Bopw0mrmzkvBVFEV5nLWiIsLgi59Xm8JvXR2fvUrSDqSm8OCzc1ZNLXdC8pagN0-LHG&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAYnTKSgsxMEmlY5jX42TlmdxRsdO1mQ30cnHDfslIROhNDyecjrtbyKlUfPAN0uhvfSsWktSyseJnZ23KFkS5XNU5pWkwHZtNOZ9MNSZ67HoEA2m_AlIx1wfz5YTecDToBjKesqfnHxz4HhWkCsC-N2FRng947u1BmwpthyKef-IFOq_-o_HJ_X54OB3muQLrAN6wStzPQ1TwPFBAZXPpbDK2VltSJt6C7SomQ9J4wWstaVhSWh9BZICbrqyshTCuAMXThO5waQ8sJxazBF2ewz8BOOthUixrJ6kKFtZlAOfLeZfmCGXdkGlK-bV-GLfg8tAVNw3xYph3aGcNJeubVXw) earlier this year.

The Lyme Bay reefs and the associated flora and fauna are considered to be nationally important conservation features and are home to many different species of marine life. Head over to our website to discover more: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/marine-life/marine-life.php](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/marine-life/marine-life.php)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_bincombe1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_bincombe1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_bincombe1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_bincombe2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_bincombe2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_bincombe2.png)

Lyme Bay fisherman and Education Outreach Ambassador Gavin Ziemann from Axmouth visited Bincombe Valley Primary School in Weymouth, Dorset this morning to present two marine litter sessions.

Each one hour session is packed with information about how marine litter ends up in the seas in around Lyme Bay, how students can play an active role in reducing marine litter along with some of the unusual objects Gavin has hauled up in his fishing nets!

If you work in education and would like a FREE visit to your school in the Devon and Dorset area please get in touch with Paula Palfrey our Education Officer:

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_superdry1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_superdry1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_superdry1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_superdry2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_superdry2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_superdry2.png)

Yesterday saw Superdry co-founder Julian Dunkerton visit [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARCSAiVM3qScwnCPbmLKiDVNqoSpzan7fD5CJ7kb5-T9j9_Qv-KASebVK9T74MVfw7XMsgWe3w1f5F6C&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARC4qO7pBsPuulATBkqqct1bmeDFPAdS2MqgvsE95ieY6UiUiIymmSvm3XvyGMYUHMN93pbUtK8RQyh8BKzADji5txDaG4Pjf5CQzQ-uxaBkPZ4CJQ-PL8XkMSoGR7PP3PD8Kir-YBVlKUBDiXQuGuuXFMUXOwe2TdVnlbqZ332ysu8Vfm5jprOQRTd0Nc0TQqNKLDsilGMa8SQE_BCMJ-aDds4L_Fo0OMTpmkzulCryQZ_59vmbMUOmRqN1PVjLZ7FrUNQ7sK2gjUR47EIZ70Vj5rpv32TUT0J5vyurWmuB673tbkIcH0wkZmjEerLShJZaW41tWNmEFoObbAGOXV5TQQ)'s Lyme Bay Reserve to see firsthand the benefits experienced by both the local fishing community and the marine environment as a result of his generous donation to BLUE earlier this year.

Julian got a real feel for the Reserve with skipper Lewis on his boat Pegasus out in Lyme Bay. They also stopped off to speak to Axmouth fisherman Angus Walker about the beneficial changes he has seen to the Reserve over the years.

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_rarefind1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_rarefind1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_rarefind1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_rarefind2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_rarefind2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_rarefind2.png)

A rare find by Lyme Regis fisherman Nigel Hill during a trip out in Lyme Bay Reserve.

Naturalist and professional wildlife photographer Steve Trewhella, based in Dorset, identified the seahorse as a male Short-snouted seahorse (Hippocampus hippocampus). According to Steve you can see the flattened pouch at the front, slightly shorter snout, and triangle like coronet on the top of the head which are the distinct marks of this particular specie of seahorse.

Want to learn more about our native Seahorses? The check out Steve's book called In the Company of Seahorses: [https://wildnaturepress.com/ou…/in-the-company-of-seahorses/](https://wildnaturepress.com/our-titles/in-the-company-of-seahorses/?fbclid=IwAR3ikvF6TMWr7nYGTO3Q_ev80KFk9Osm7K5h5wIpINDKjIlqeMm62xGpk2s)

Photos by Nigel Hill and Steve Trewhella.

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

Fantastic film from [BBC Earth](https://www.facebook.com/bbcearth/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARCTaQ1n5iS5LdPzKWhciMKoaYUehR4k9stOn1JIubw6A_amxNLU94iALHVsh0o_3DT1PcjGrU4rQfSZ&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAp_2WnuOtCH7M50FG7hF6Gi9H_Y3AI7fna9dZ5yQbO6mVxveUdBn9jaqwcQ5uXIeqC2A0tXej1S2gh9k8yt5pLCcreBODXh18o4zEPnF5mQG368veYJylUx9sbhMRuTUV-00KivZDRNepx-3-jjfSJ4I-SogXqMIQZD7_c91Q6CDCdUCAvPRF-gUM7UxbdyVi3Zeo2xRVVhJNOTDXBDnsSt5NUHwgz7N1-fMrzsVrHt7O9r5wprtp7B_k8YwE1oAE1kM0fzOorlv_6uCYh0AK8DGUqTEUpu528tiLnP4Z3O31-R7aUNgLBrHVwMpnTEEramRTvfJ1WOQI5qb5r-qry4m1NZFhSHm5xB_7Zl6Yj1iZXAElMml-vEaZqE7EiTGUMNDoGikjZVBZMoYirbefnMMuaoHZbxRqNOoES_kpmXzcUHN5xKaHBTZQ0wROQdZ-Aa7g_crzq3WQdBUFo_oFzoJwHxw-E8-mF-XxyC_ee4sw0W7sna34-U2FsdQYOAL3945QXSyAEpLUQR4LzkzzkdAbYj07ZN5MJ1ghVI2hyvrKnGNvt7HyezuPuGeKybNA2t9c8jenRUs780t2QvtParLoWlQ9TLodub1i0VSuiSovuPZW4be-0pako7u-bsnsjFAio8fRIragg1bExnmQXQ9nrdXuR_bDL1OHib6t9) [#OurBluePlanet](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/ourblueplanet?source=feed_text&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAp_2WnuOtCH7M50FG7hF6Gi9H_Y3AI7fna9dZ5yQbO6mVxveUdBn9jaqwcQ5uXIeqC2A0tXej1S2gh9k8yt5pLCcreBODXh18o4zEPnF5mQG368veYJylUx9sbhMRuTUV-00KivZDRNepx-3-jjfSJ4I-SogXqMIQZD7_c91Q6CDCdUCAvPRF-gUM7UxbdyVi3Zeo2xRVVhJNOTDXBDnsSt5NUHwgz7N1-fMrzsVrHt7O9r5wprtp7B_k8YwE1oAE1kM0fzOorlv_6uCYh0AK8DGUqTEUpu528tiLnP4Z3O31-R7aUNgLBrHVwMpnTEEramRTvfJ1WOQI5qb5r-qry4m1NZFhSHm5xB_7Zl6Yj1iZXAElMml-vEaZqE7EiTGUMNDoGikjZVBZMoYirbefnMMuaoHZbxRqNOoES_kpmXzcUHN5xKaHBTZQ0wROQdZ-Aa7g_crzq3WQdBUFo_oFzoJwHxw-E8-mF-XxyC_ee4sw0W7sna34-U2FsdQYOAL3945QXSyAEpLUQR4LzkzzkdAbYj07ZN5MJ1ghVI2hyvrKnGNvt7HyezuPuGeKybNA2t9c8jenRUs780t2QvtParLoWlQ9TLodub1i0VSuiSovuPZW4be-0pako7u-bsnsjFAio8fRIragg1bExnmQXQ9nrdXuR_bDL1OHib6t9&__tn__=%2ANK-R) showing that with strong collaboration between fishers, conservationists and managers we can build sustainable fisheries.

Great work by Dr Emma Sheehan from [University of Plymouth](https://www.facebook.com/plymouthuni/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARDobZaWb_g3QhbCiYw4WLNDlWhG0z-HfpmXIXcS3WxQZhqf7f_FCY0RpILrCjLIxaprITY4czKqI51x&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAp_2WnuOtCH7M50FG7hF6Gi9H_Y3AI7fna9dZ5yQbO6mVxveUdBn9jaqwcQ5uXIeqC2A0tXej1S2gh9k8yt5pLCcreBODXh18o4zEPnF5mQG368veYJylUx9sbhMRuTUV-00KivZDRNepx-3-jjfSJ4I-SogXqMIQZD7_c91Q6CDCdUCAvPRF-gUM7UxbdyVi3Zeo2xRVVhJNOTDXBDnsSt5NUHwgz7N1-fMrzsVrHt7O9r5wprtp7B_k8YwE1oAE1kM0fzOorlv_6uCYh0AK8DGUqTEUpu528tiLnP4Z3O31-R7aUNgLBrHVwMpnTEEramRTvfJ1WOQI5qb5r-qry4m1NZFhSHm5xB_7Zl6Yj1iZXAElMml-vEaZqE7EiTGUMNDoGikjZVBZMoYirbefnMMuaoHZbxRqNOoES_kpmXzcUHN5xKaHBTZQ0wROQdZ-Aa7g_crzq3WQdBUFo_oFzoJwHxw-E8-mF-XxyC_ee4sw0W7sna34-U2FsdQYOAL3945QXSyAEpLUQR4LzkzzkdAbYj07ZN5MJ1ghVI2hyvrKnGNvt7HyezuPuGeKybNA2t9c8jenRUs780t2QvtParLoWlQ9TLodub1i0VSuiSovuPZW4be-0pako7u-bsnsjFAio8fRIragg1bExnmQXQ9nrdXuR_bDL1OHib6t9) Marine Institute, the [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARDYoe_DULJdjFq_L7Pc5e1HVXh6u6aUBAlRqeq38XhrmeXpBS816hccsZ3xvc3BLpllNmhPb-uOCymR&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAp_2WnuOtCH7M50FG7hF6Gi9H_Y3AI7fna9dZ5yQbO6mVxveUdBn9jaqwcQ5uXIeqC2A0tXej1S2gh9k8yt5pLCcreBODXh18o4zEPnF5mQG368veYJylUx9sbhMRuTUV-00KivZDRNepx-3-jjfSJ4I-SogXqMIQZD7_c91Q6CDCdUCAvPRF-gUM7UxbdyVi3Zeo2xRVVhJNOTDXBDnsSt5NUHwgz7N1-fMrzsVrHt7O9r5wprtp7B_k8YwE1oAE1kM0fzOorlv_6uCYh0AK8DGUqTEUpu528tiLnP4Z3O31-R7aUNgLBrHVwMpnTEEramRTvfJ1WOQI5qb5r-qry4m1NZFhSHm5xB_7Zl6Yj1iZXAElMml-vEaZqE7EiTGUMNDoGikjZVBZMoYirbefnMMuaoHZbxRqNOoES_kpmXzcUHN5xKaHBTZQ0wROQdZ-Aa7g_crzq3WQdBUFo_oFzoJwHxw-E8-mF-XxyC_ee4sw0W7sna34-U2FsdQYOAL3945QXSyAEpLUQR4LzkzzkdAbYj07ZN5MJ1ghVI2hyvrKnGNvt7HyezuPuGeKybNA2t9c8jenRUs780t2QvtParLoWlQ9TLodub1i0VSuiSovuPZW4be-0pako7u-bsnsjFAio8fRIragg1bExnmQXQ9nrdXuR_bDL1OHib6t9) and the fishers from Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve.

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_catherine1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_catherine1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_catherine1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_catherine2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_catherine2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_catherine2.png)

Catherine Whitley, UK Researcher from the [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARATfWtGB2GHHlR0b3ifTPrk5v8oIQJctCdnRYbTtQGeyveEmietdSzw0gU9O4Ilwx0Udgwz0DPAnvlW&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAC5DILpIQt_hOeMgl9SJ306pF8sTVqNMTnDctyuq0qIDHgfLjnzH2fFGv41OLsXjDGFALpeQ3RIDPni_x5X3itQirm5o7DL7UfAcvoks34Vn8RuK2ltvFxFaOKdvGuUXRZ7BpmIsZcCDSEo0Ww3-U2tHOaVcFolM20RroLUE2DIZT2jD0OmwzBIAyPQ2ndcAfO55TcJHZtIWvSG1Fjh51vhs5f2V9nGUqREUllDmHf7KZQqvb_uFRFm2kUu9t28iyC9yJSxE9gOXbZOYBH1xU2CQqAwcTRv7QQD6a5dScxD2Acjx1q8chQGftoLBVUBvAxIeyrw6MsvQuJGZyon6KEpw) (facilitators of the Lyme Bay Reserve) presented to recreational sea anglers on Wednesday evening about the ray project being conducted in Lyme Bay Reserve.

It was an opportunity for sea anglers to feedback to Catherine observations regarding recent catch landings of rays and to discover more about the Reserve.

If you are a sea angler fishing in the Lyme Bay area we encourage you to get in touch with Catherine about your ray observations. Emails to:

And if anyone would like Catherine to present at their next sea angling club meeting she'd be more than happy to!

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_education1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_education1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_education1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_education2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_education2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_education2.png)

It's been a busy week for our Education Outreach Team with yet another school visit, this time to Combe Martin Sea School in Devon presenting the Marine Litter presentation in the school assembly.

There is so much to learn about the impact litter has on our seas in and around Lyme Bay Reserve and the stories our fishermen tell of the weird and wonderful objects they have found whilst out fishing.

Great work [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARB2SIUsxDB_1b-WOEe34AKcRWTWBM1Fm4J8q82Ekf0SDMxm7WMlk0PCRJEsDfvP_pdYuwR7atZIc7al&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARALvyoB_WyG8fx29yS1DcLe9uBO4sfB53IfqNs0x2a5Pi_QRO4Eq9xeJlw8TsYbnfIKsVt3wVkTGuQ52OwE4CBx-W9FRCHHEJrwfLt5B_rUpy9CoSH93QQ_qrnHy8LYSX9A-zXkl2YlHi2Rl0ALBWdkZD-QuB3q4Soe43uGd7LyHD3UIlSL3_argCbvNvKRK-9ex8Qs9BKkKlOSyBaKi6er1_3bUyZLAG3BpjLEic_xSWraLA1DXFm_Zz38mditbbxCW-g-gfhralcB8x2ORTQd4dr4q-sQ26tc3oR1GXbELQ1AAwzmE1JNHqGfy1TypbyvGKxY-nsj9RXziYdJI5A5rA) Education Team!

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_barton.png)](images/large_news_nov18_barton.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_barton.png)

We headed to Bradley Barton Primary School in Newton Abbot, Devon yesterday to present our sustainable fishing presentation to Year 2.

As part of our presentation we invite children up to take part in the interactive fishing game which demonstrates how different net sizes affects how many fish are caught. Great fun for everyone, including our Fisherman Ambassador Gavin Ziemann from the fishing port of Axmouth, Devon (pictured).

If you work in education and would like a FREE visit to your school we are currently offering two presentations; 1) Sustainable Fishing and 2) Marine Litter.

Get in touch with our Education Officer Paula Palfrey to make your booking:

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_dinner1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_dinner1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_dinner1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_dinner2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_dinner2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_dinner2.png)

Last week we held an exclusive dinner celebrating a new collaboration with [Roast Restaurant](https://www.facebook.com/RoastRestaurantUK/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARCqV_pCj8CcQJDJAW8THIRVkDDpKYjPDF2LYbO-8jsluWS8qlxSaTgrBIuekw7_Yptq9zov56FNHcSS&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAxmOCti-E4S48erJ13ZIhP0bgfGwEbriEAuRP3JmyLdznKlRb1hjC8PK55fwvrJug6bmXMNnGr7DY3hbN9dCqEVzoF4CP6r068X_tgwWW1lO_wPTh33hWKY-riFkeZNpEC2T2UVb7WbweTccnxw6oBRl0f1G4vBi5n33BUPK92lZs4gvaq8rPQ20wSSZuCqV3AwQZZiHMwsvBDd0eFiWMZiZX9FEgZqEyeAr5m6ZjtVHqpxRUDs2HA7EDOYEy0Yb3YtyDLDO4JT50by2aDMid-6Sd7rGG2Cf2MpamXuAq5qhjQrridcVVJIfIN-cG8elxCtASfJIJzgJ57INqWGYJ3Sg) and the Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve, facilitated by the [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARDbcRVkBKVrWa7TsrUzLyKmL_0jGTnd_cNFOH7Kj6VrDYK2YJZUZ0M4DiL0YsI9_BlpzAqZmoRVSXZ2&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAxmOCti-E4S48erJ13ZIhP0bgfGwEbriEAuRP3JmyLdznKlRb1hjC8PK55fwvrJug6bmXMNnGr7DY3hbN9dCqEVzoF4CP6r068X_tgwWW1lO_wPTh33hWKY-riFkeZNpEC2T2UVb7WbweTccnxw6oBRl0f1G4vBi5n33BUPK92lZs4gvaq8rPQ20wSSZuCqV3AwQZZiHMwsvBDd0eFiWMZiZX9FEgZqEyeAr5m6ZjtVHqpxRUDs2HA7EDOYEy0Yb3YtyDLDO4JT50by2aDMid-6Sd7rGG2Cf2MpamXuAq5qhjQrridcVVJIfIN-cG8elxCtASfJIJzgJ57INqWGYJ3Sg).

Showcasing fish freshly caught by the fishermen of Lyme Bay Fisheries and Conservation Reserve expertly prepared by Lyme Bay local Paul Shearing, Executive Chef at London's Roast Restaurant. Among his guests were the fishermen who caught the fish in Lyme Bay Reserve earlier that day!

If you are interested in this sustainable, premium quality, provenance-assured seafood from Lyme Bay visit: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_fresh1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_fresh1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_fresh1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_fresh2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_fresh2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_fresh2.png)

Plaice and flounder season is upon us in Lyme Bay Reserve. Our flat fish are caught sustainably using static nets and head straight to Plymouth Trawler Agents ready for market.

This is the best time of year to enjoy this creamy, versatile flatfish caught by local fishermen from Lyme Bay. Eat local and support Lyme Bay fishermen.

For more details about Reserve Seafood head over to: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_spinylobster.png)](images/large_news_nov18_spinylobster.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_spinylobster.png)

Lyme Regis fisherman Nigel Hill caught a spiny lobster a few weeks ago. The spiny lobster is thought to be making a comeback across the South West and over in the Channel Islands. They were wiped out by overfishing in the 80s so this is a really exciting find.

Commercially caught species within Lyme Bay Reserve are many and diverse and the Lyme Bay reefs and associated flora and fauna are considered to be nationally important conservation features.

Head over to our website to discover more about this wonderful and diverse area: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/marine-life/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/marine-life/)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_reserveseafood.png)](images/large_news_nov18_reserveseafood.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_reserveseafood.png)

Reserve Seafood is a sustainable, premium quality, provenance-assured product from Lyme Bay and is a result of the fishermen's dedication to catch quality.

29 species of seafood were sold through Reserve Seafood in 2017 with top species including; crab, lobster, pollack, turbot and dover sole.

For more information about Reserve Seafood and where to buy visit: [www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/](http://www.lymebayreserve.co.uk/reserve-seafood/)

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_ray1.png)](images/large_news_nov18_ray1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_ray1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_nov18_ray2.png)](images/large_news_nov18_ray2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_nov18_ray2.png)

Catherine Whitley, UK Researcher from [Blue Marine Foundation](https://www.facebook.com/Bluemarinef/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARD-vqXYMLE_er5s3aDuYnqhHNo4MgMQ3y8Saz2K0hA5NDC-4_INH4CpjwSmu83j24b2iFfQHvTELL6F&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAy3zLyk6DJC8WK3TnKrdrIPaEi76DWk8Gid_Xh7zt1LaCCFQG_UKVrZTnEidP1yG7ttwL8LCPXCeUsWr2gLXJokgt-skzOCMLeiLVRndALchfxM0Bx-ZL9evpOWcYxXYW8ng3oKUSIs0WjD5-h8gbItysxwFu7qc8uMzzJsGeOCwvjCutn00t3X6BbHZCUDwG8JbFOj8Hii8cxd_R9JyoBDXERLS4_atGd-eLiyx5-R49HXakKhPxIN-5GY5ZXE8pU0F3WOB9v1se4hI7MLoc31bw0Mc13n9HH12bibMhT3ZMfPOR0ekVwmXr6j40PdhapBz4xAj72rQIMHwOsojiHaJvxEMyMR_ESjFVWF7Eyeeipjl6yBPZMUAk2rjxcu0emC6jNCHtZB97mqtaFrLidpHHxnmASGsFh3UTg29oT5lHdnjxeiKFgajVN8DTpDAkaXv7E_q1AK32EQrnHKrXntvykgCIjMmvuzjN8oy7DqoBo7cN8zpBW) (facilitators of Lyme Bay Reserve) went along to [Honiton Sea Angling Club](https://www.facebook.com/Honitonsac/?__tn__=K-R&eid=ARCHjfx2LPM_L3GkDb55-acFUfVVTfCUJ09rBI1w0I1ByMIGLB_d85d6ad4PQOJssbRyTgxCNjTDM_oY&fref=mentions&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAy3zLyk6DJC8WK3TnKrdrIPaEi76DWk8Gid_Xh7zt1LaCCFQG_UKVrZTnEidP1yG7ttwL8LCPXCeUsWr2gLXJokgt-skzOCMLeiLVRndALchfxM0Bx-ZL9evpOWcYxXYW8ng3oKUSIs0WjD5-h8gbItysxwFu7qc8uMzzJsGeOCwvjCutn00t3X6BbHZCUDwG8JbFOj8Hii8cxd_R9JyoBDXERLS4_atGd-eLiyx5-R49HXakKhPxIN-5GY5ZXE8pU0F3WOB9v1se4hI7MLoc31bw0Mc13n9HH12bibMhT3ZMfPOR0ekVwmXr6j40PdhapBz4xAj72rQIMHwOsojiHaJvxEMyMR_ESjFVWF7Eyeeipjl6yBPZMUAk2rjxcu0emC6jNCHtZB97mqtaFrLidpHHxnmASGsFh3UTg29oT5lHdnjxeiKFgajVN8DTpDAkaXv7E_q1AK32EQrnHKrXntvykgCIjMmvuzjN8oy7DqoBo7cN8zpBW) last week.

She gave an overview of the Reserve and its 10 year history and also explained how anglers can help her collect data for her study on catches of rays in the Reserve with a new app that has been developed.

The study aims to assess the spatial distribution, capture methods and discard survivorship of these key commercial species to inform appropriate management measures within the Reserve.

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

[![](images/photo_news_oct18_rayresearch1.png)](images/large_news_oct18_rayresearch1.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_oct18_rayresearch1.png)

[![](images/photo_news_oct18_rayresearch2.png)](images/large_news_oct18_rayresearch2.jpg)

![](images/photo_news_oct18_rayresearch2.png)

Catherine Whitley, UK Researcher at the Blue Marine Foundation (facilitators of Lyme Bay Reserve) spent the day out at sea last week with Lyme Bay fisherman Gavin Ziemann measuring any rays he caught as part of her study.

The study is researching into the sustainability of the local ray fishery in Lyme Bay Reserve. This study aims to assess the spatial distribution, capture methods and discard survivorship of these key commercial species to inform appropriate management measures within the Reserve.

![](/images/line_hr_mid.png)

Lyme Bay Reserve Case Studies ... The Lyme Bay model demonstrates how well-managed marine protected areas can restore ecosystems and support local communities.

enriched_points.expanded_content: The Lyme Bay Fisheries Reserve, established through local fisherman collaboration, successfully revitalized marine ecosystems while ensuring sustainable fishery practices, benefiting both marine life and the fishing community.

Ra’ui, a traditional marine conservation method in the Cook Islands, empowers local communities to manage and protect marine resources, ensuring long-term sustainability and biodiversity preservation.

Successful fisheries management hinges on community engagement, adaptive practices, and collaboration among stakeholders, fostering sustainable ecosystems and resilient fishing communities, as evidenced by Lyme Bay and Ra’ui.
